properties.title: 2 bedroom detached house for sale
properties.fullDescription: *UNIQUE HIGH-END CONVERSION*TWO SELF-CONTAINED UNITS*FLEXIBLE LIVING*INCOME POTENTIAL*UNDERFLOOR HEATING & SOLAR PV*OFF-STREET PARKING & GARDEN*BI-FOLD DOORS*POPULAR NORTH TYNE TOWN OF BELLINGHAM* Discover Plashetts & The Coach House at Parkside Mews, a remarkable and unique high-end conversion offering two stunning self-contained units, perfectly situated in the popular North Tyne town of Bellingham. This versatile property presents an exceptional opportunity for flexible multi-generational living, dedicated guest accommodation, or a compelling income stream. The main residence, The Coach House, exudes contemporary elegance with its thoughtful design. Enter into a bright, open-plan living space with a stunning contemporary kitchen, complete with modern appliances. Bi-fold doors enhance the connection to the outdoors, creating an ideal environment for relaxation and enjoyment. The well-sized double bedroom benefits from an en-suite bathroom and a separate, spacious dressing area, ensuring comfort and convenience. Both units benefit from underfloor oil-fired heating, with a shared boiler system. The Coach House also benefits from the efficiency of solar PV panels located on the roof of Plashetts, providing electricity generation. Adjacent, Plashetts provides a charming self-contained counterpart, offering well-proportioned and finished contemporary accommodation. This unit includes a comfortable reception room, a modern kitchen, a double bedroom and a well-appointed showerroom and ensuite W/C. This arrangement offers superb independence for guests or additional occupants. Externally, the property boasts off-street parking and a driveway. Both units share access to convenient outdoor spaces, including a well maintained garden and courtyard garden, offering tranquil areas for alfresco dining or quiet contemplation. Within the garden is large timber storage shed with lighting and power and an outside water tap. This chain-free property delivers versatile accommodation in a desirable area, combining period charm with modern comforts. Its thoughtful conversion and dual-unit setup make it a truly distinctive offering in Bellingham. SERVICES Mains electricity, mains water connected. Heating via underfloor oil-fired system (shared boiler). Solar PV for electricity generation (on Plashetts roof). The properties are also pre-wired for use with a self sufficient generator. TENURE Freehold DIRECTIONS WHAT3WORDS: Every three metre square of the world has been given a unique combination of three words. private.spurted.encloses REFERRAL FEES In accordance with the Estate Agents’ (Provision of Information) Regulations 1991 and the Consumer Protection from Unfair Trading Regulations 2008, we are obliged to inform you that this Company may offer the following services to sellers and purchasers from which we may earn a related referral fee from on completion, in particular the referral of: Conveyancing where typically we can receive an average fee of £100.00 plus VA

properties.title: 3 bed property for sale
properties.description: a central position within the highly desirable Norh Tyne village of Bellingham, this charming stone-built ...Listed on 30th Jun 2026CharacterOff Street Parking
properties.fullDescription: Occupying a central position within the highly desirable Norh Tyne village of Bellingham, this charming stone-built mid-terrace cottage offers a rare opportunity to acquire a beautifully refurbished home steeped in history. Potentially formerly part of the village granary and partially Grade II Listed, the property successfully combines period character with stylish modern living with necessary upgrades having been done by the current owners.The accommodation is arranged over three floors and has been tastefully updated throughout while retaining many of its original features and unique charm. The ground floor comprises a welcoming spacious lounge, a separate dining room ideal for entertaining, a useful home office, well-appointed kitchen and a ground floor WC for extra convenience. Excellent storage has been thoughtfully incorporated throughout the property, adding to its superb practicality.To the first floor are two generous king size bedrooms and a family bathroom, all presented to a high standard. A further staircase from the landing leads to the second-floor loft conversion, creating an impressive third bedroom or studio space with characterful features and a versatile layout. The property offers plenty of features to please all palates including exposed stone and beams, fireplaces and ceiling heights.Externally, the property benefits from driveway parking for two cars, a valuable asset within the village centre, together with attractive lawned garden and extended patio areas that provide ideal spaces for outdoor dining, entertaining and relaxation.As mentioned above, lots of work has been done to the property in recent years including windows, doors, roof and heating system giving buyers peace of mind.Situated within walking distance of Bellingham's excellent range of local amenities, including shops, cafés, pubs and leisure facilities, this unique home offers the perfect blend of character, convenience and modern comfort.Properties of this nature and historical significance rarely become available on the open market, and an early viewing is strongly recommended to avoid disappointment.Internal dimensionsKitchen: 18'4 max x 9'4 max (5.59m x 2.84m)Lounge: 18'6 into alcove x 14'4 max (5.64m x 4.37m)Dining Room: 14'5 max x 9'8 max (4.39m x 2.95m)Study: 6'5 max x 5'2 max (1.96m x 1.57m)Bedroom One: 15'1 into alcove x 14'5 max (4.60m x 4.39m)Bedroom Two: 12'9 into alcove x 14'8 max (3.89m x 4.47m)Bathroom: 18'5 max x 6'4 max (5.61m x 1.93m)Bedroom Three: 16'7 plus recess x 10'0 max (5.05m x 3.05m)Stone Outhouse: 6'1 x 4'3 (1.85m x 1.3m)Primary services supplyElectricity: MainsWater: MainsSewerage: MainsHeating: OilBroadband: Fibre to CabinetMobile Signal Coverage Blackspot: NoParking: DrivewayMiningThe North East region is famous for its rich mining heritage and therefore it will be beneficial to conduct a mining search. properties.fullDescription: SummaryOffer with no onward chain, Pattinson are delighted to offer for sale a delightful 3-bedroom terraced house tucked away in the lushness of the semi-rural Bellingham area, Hexham. This characterful property successfully blends traditional aesthetics with modern comforts, making it a perfect abode for individuals who wish to enjoy countryside charm without sacrificing essential amenities.The dwelling offers a thoughtfully designed floor plan spread over two floors. The ground floor is home to two spacious reception rooms, offering an ideal setting for both family time as well as entertaining. Thanks to larger windows, each room is bathed in natural light during the day and offers stunning views of the nearby greenery. The kitchen is well-equipped with essential appliances, worktops and ample storage space offering plenty of room for culinary exploration. The heart of the property is the fantastic kitchen-diner, with ample storage and a stunning range cooker, meaning cooking and entertaining can combine in a cosy atmosphere.On the first floor, you'll find three inviting bedrooms each exuding a serene atmosphere perfect for relaxation, further complemented by the family bathroom's modern fixtures and fittings.The property is rated EPC D and falls into Council Tax Band B, making it a cost-effective option for buyers. Additionally, you'll get to enjoy the pristine, semi-rural location that is Bellingham, providing the serenity and peace often found missing in bustling city lives. Despite the rural feel, the house is just a short drive away from Bellingham and its local shops, restaurants, schools, and other amenities, providing you with the best of both worlds.Immerse yourself in the charm of rural living with this fantastic opportunity in Bellingham, Hexham. Contact Pattinson Estate Agents today for more information or to arrange a viewing. properties.fullDescription: This Recently Refurbished Cottage At East Woodburn Is For Sale With No Onward Chain!Located in the tranquil village of East Woodburn, Hexham, this two-bedroom terrace cottage offers a superb opportunity. Renovated to a high standard throughout, this property blends traditional character with modern comforts, making it ideal for a holiday home investment or a permanent residence.Upon entry, the deceptively spacious layout is evident. The living area features an impressive large fireplace, a captivating focal point that promises cosy evenings and a warm atmosphere. This central feature enhances the cottage's charm and provides a wonderful space for relaxation and entertaining. The comprehensive renovation ensures a comfortable and stylish living experience. The property includes one well-appointed bathroom and a single reception room, providing ample space for daily living. The design maximises natural light throughout.A bright conservatory to the rear extends the living accommodation. This versatile space is perfect for enjoying garden views, dining, or unwinding, regardless of the weather. Beyond the conservatory lies a private garden, offering a peaceful outdoor sanctuary. East Woodburn provides an idyllic rural location, a serene escape with excellent connectivity. The property benefits from convenient access to the A68, facilitating straightforward travel to Northumberland's stunning landscapes and the historic Scottish Borders. This strategic location makes it an attractive base for exploring the region's natural beauty, charming market towns, and numerous attractions.This cottage is offered for sale with no onward chain, simplifying the purchasing process for a smoother, quicker transaction. Whether seeking a lucrative holiday let investment or a peaceful, beautifully finished home, this East Woodburn cottage represents a unique and appealing proposition. properties.description: This cottage is a real gem offering peaceful rural life with green views, plus a small paddock which takes you down through ...Listed on 17th Nov 2025With LandSouth Facing
properties.fullDescription: This cottage is a real gem offering peaceful rural life with green views, plus a small paddock which takes you down through woodland to the banks of the Smales burn.No 2 Smalesmouth Cottage is located in an area that contains some of the darkest skies in Europe. Step out and walk up the fell for 800 metres to the edge of the giant Kielder forest, which actually began its life here with initial planting in 1926. Only a mile or so away is the spectacular Kielder water, with its terrific views, along with walking and cycling tracks. Plus, shops and a restaurant.The Dark Sky Observatory nestling in the vast swathes of the unique and magnificent countryside of the north Tyne valley can transport you to the stars! A short walk from the cottage takes you to the banks of the North Tyne River. Nearby the village of Falstone has a pub and a tearoom, and there is also a pub and restaurant with accommodation in Stannersburn, very close to the cottage.A 15 minute drive takes you to Bellingham where there is an array of amenities including petrol station, shops, pharmacy, doctors surgery as well as both primary and middle Schools. There is a further primary school at Greenhaugh.Viewings are strongly recommended to appreciate the location, space and lifestyle on offer. Worth noting, the other half of this semi-detached cottage is also available for sale.Internal dimensionsKitchen: 7'5 x 11'3 (2.26m x 3.43m)Lounge: 15'11 x 12'9 (4.85m x 3.89m)Bathroom: 7'7 x 6'1 (2.31m x 1.85m)WC: 5'7 x 2'11 (1.70m x 0.89m)Bedroom One: 15'11 x 9'11 (4.85m x 3.02m)Bedroom Two: 15'10 x 9'11 reduced height (4.83m x 3.02m)Bedroom Three: 16'0 x 15'10 into alcove (4.88m x 4.83m)Primary services supplyElectricity: MainsWater: PrivateSewerage: Septic TankHeating: OilBroadband: NoneMobile Signal Coverage Blackspot: NoParking: Allocated SpaceMiningWe are unaware of any works affecting this property however the North East region is famous for its rich mining heritage and therefore it will be beneficial to conduct a mining search. properties.description: An exceptional redevelopment opportunity comprising a former social club, an attached residential cottage, and extensive ...Listed on 18th Apr 2026Call
properties.fullDescription: DescriptionSituated in the historic village of Otterburn, the property is located within a largely residential area and is a short walk from the village centre.Key Features* Site Area: Approximately 1 acre.* Location: Bordering the Northumberland National Park and Kielder Forest Park.* Tenure: Freehold with vacant possession.* Services: Connected to mains water, electricity, and drainage.The Property & GroundsThe former social club building itself has been used for alternative business purposes, whilst the attached cottage/bungalow is in disrepair.The site is bounded by housing on three sides, while the eastern aspect offers a picturesque outlook over a wooded dene. A tributary of the River Rede runs along the boundary, featuring a waterfall near the north-east corner of the site.The site is split level which could provide an excellent opportunity for a mix of apartments and houses, subject to planning, with the apartments having exceptional River views.Location:Otterburn is a historic village located approximately 30 miles north-west of Newcastle upon Tyne. Positioned on a primary route to the Scottish Borders, the village serves as a gateway to Northumberland's most famous natural landmarks and provides a range of essential local amenities. From just a 3 minute walk away from Northumberland's award winning wedding venue Le Petit Chateau, there is high demand for short term holiday accommodation, as well as demand for properties from a variety of end users.Unconditional offers are invited for the freehold interest and early viewing is recommended.This land is being sold on behalf of family members. All legally authorised owners have provided consent for the land to be marketed. A formal written declaration of this personal interest will be provided to all prospective purchasers prior to any negotiations. properties.fullDescription: We welcome to the market this beautiful, detached home, originally built circa 1750 as a counting-house, now offering spacious accommodation in a superb rural setting with period features such as exposed beams and original fireplaces. Sitting within a plot of approximately half an acre, this home near Otterburn offers generous outside space with uninterrupted panoramic views of the Northumberland countryside. This is a peaceful retreat in natural surroundings, but with good road links providing access to surrounding towns for amenities, being set astride the A68.The internal accommodation comprises entrance vestibule; through to a sitting room, a great room for entertaining with exposed beams; a spacious living room with a traditional exposed brick feature fireplace and the first of two staircases to the first floor; kitchen/diner boasting a range of fitted base, drawer and wall units in a chic wood finish; sunroom perfect to relax in with surrounding windows flooding in natural light and offering woodland views; a spacious dining room with second staircase to the first floor; utility room; and a convenient downstairs W.C.The first floor can be accessed by either of the two staircases. These lead to two separate landings, each giving access to two bedrooms and a family bathroom. All four bedrooms are double with space for bedroom furniture and offer tasteful décor and fitted wardrobes for extra storage. properties.fullDescription: Nestled within the peaceful hamlet of Stannersburn is a charming countryside home offering the perfect balance of character, originality, comfort, and rural tranquillity. Surrounded by the stunning Northumberland landscape, this delightful cottage provides an idyllic retreat for those seeking a slower pace of life while remaining within easy reach of local amenities and excellent walking, cycling, and outdoor pursuits.Beautifully presented throughout, the property combines traditional cottage charm with practical modern living. The welcoming interior offers bright and well-proportioned accommodation, featuring three cosy living spaces with character features, a well-appointed kitchen, and two comfortable bedrooms with lots of fitted storage, designed to make the most of the peaceful surroundings. Dual aspect windows allow natural light to flood the home while framing picturesque countryside views.A light filled porch leads through to the entrance hallway giving access to the kitchen, ground floor shower room and the main reception space, as well as the stairs to the first floor.The kitchen is well equipped with plenty of wall and base units, worktops, a storage cupboard utilising the space beneath the stairs, integrated appliances, and a breakfast bar taking advantage of the stunning views.The ground floor shower room is a great size and comprises a large shower cubicle, WC, hand basin, and heated towel rail.The three reception rooms are connected. The dining room is the central room of the ground floor, housing a fabulous multi-fuel burning stove and featuring original timber paneling.The lounge is spacious and is situated off the dining room, benefiting from another multi-fuel burning stove which takes centre stage. The end of the room is fully glazed from wall to wall, bringing in plenty of natural light through from the conservatory.With its elevated position, the outlook over the gardens from the conservatory is very pleasant on all three sides.Upstairs are two spacious bedrooms with fitted storage and a very well-equipped family bathroom. The main bedroom is dual aspect and very generous in size, offering a full range of fitted wardrobes as well as an attached walk-in dressing room.There is plenty of parking at the property on the driveway, on-street outside the property, and in the attached garage.The garage is a tremendous space for parking, storage or hobbies; however, it also offers the opportunity to expand the accommodation into the garage space (subject to the usual consents).Externally, the property enjoys a lovely setting with plenty of outdoor space, recently extended with the purchase of additional garden space, ideal for relaxing, entertaining, or simply enjoying the surrounding scenery and wildlife. The quiet village location provides a true sense of escape, making East Cottage perfectly suited as a permanent residence, holiday home, or investment opportunity.Stannersburn is well placed for exploring the wider Northumberland Na

properties.fullDescription: We are pleased to offer to the market this exceptional three-bedroom detached cottage offering over 2,200 sq.ft. Of beautifully presented, character-filled accommodation in the heart of the picturesque Northumberland village of Kirkwhelpington. Rich in period charm yet thoughtfully updated for modern living, this distinctive stone-built home is surrounded by idyllic wraparound gardens and benefits from solar panels, a versatile outbuilding, and a generous cobble-style driveway providing ample off-street parking.Upon entering through the wrought-iron gate into the stone-walled front garden, the property exudes warmth and welcome. Established planting and climbing ivy frame the entrance, creating an inviting approach. Inside, the sense of space becomes immediately apparent. At the heart of the home lies an impressive 23-ft kitchen and breakfast room, fitted with shaker-style cabinetry, rich wooden countertops, and a central island ideal for informal dining and everyday gathering. A traditional range cooker enhances the rustic aesthetic, while modern conveniences ensure the space functions perfectly for contemporary life.The formal dining room, has dual-aspect windows, and provides an elegant setting for gatherings and special occasions. From the cosy yet generously proportioned living room, glazed doors open into a striking octagonal conservatory, a bright and tranquil retreat that enjoys uninterrupted views across the lush landscaped gardens. The ground floor also includes a versatile fourth bedroom, currently utilised as a study, offering excellent flexibility for home working or guest accommodation. Completing this level is a stylish family bathroom featuring a freestanding bath and separate shower, along with a cloakroom and practical storage areas.Upstairs, the property continues to impress with three spacious double bedrooms, two benefitting from its own ensuite facilities. The principal bedroom is particularly inviting, offering generous proportions and a peaceful atmosphere. A smartly finished shower room and separate WC also serve the upper floor, adding thoughtful convenience to the layout.Blending rustic charm with modern functionality, Ingledew is a much-loved and beautifully maintained home with a rare combination of generous living space, elegant period features, and superb outdoor areas. Its solar panels, useful outbuilding, and extensive landscaped gardens make it an ideal retreat for those seeking a unique village home full of character in an idyllic Northumberland setting.Additional information:Council Tax Band: ELocal Authority: NorthumberlandEarly viewing is highly recommended due to the property being realistically priced.Disclaimer:These particulars, whilst believed to be accurate are set out as a general guideline and do not constitute any part of an offer or contract. properties.title: 2 bed property for sale
properties.address: Wall Methodist Chapel, The Chare, Wall, Hexham, Northumberland NE46
properties.description: A distinguished Grade II Listed chapel offering huge potential for residential conversion or for use as superb ...Listed on 10th Jul 2025
properties.fullDescription: Accommodation in BriefEntrance Lobby | Office Space 1 | Office Space 2 with Kitchenette | Toilets | External Storage SpaceThe PropertyPositioned at the heart of the village of Wall, overlooking the green and surrounded by glorious Northumberland countryside, this handsome Grade II Listed former Methodist Chapel presents a rare and versatile opportunity. Dating from 1868 and built in traditional stone and slate, the building offers elegantly proportioned, characterful spaces that have been beautifully maintained and is ideally suited to a variety of commercial uses or, subject to the necessary consents, an inspiring canvas for residential conversion.The Chapel has undergone thoughtful refurbishment, including an electrical re-wire, with historic elements carefully conserved throughout. Internally, its layout offers a natural flow and flexibility, with soaring ceilings and abundant natural light. An entrance lobby leads into the first of two generous office spaces, known as The Chapel, which retains the building’s original sense of scale and presence features a striking open-plan space framed by arched sash windows.Double doors connect through to The Studio - a further large, light-filled room that would lend itself equally to workspace, gallery use, studio, or reception space. This room also opens into a kitchenette, where integrated bench seating is paired with modern fittings, including a dishwasher, under-counter fridge, portable induction hob, smart oven, and electric hot water tap.To one side, ancillary facilities include two well-appointed WC cubicles and a washbasin, with plumbing and wiring in place for an electric shower if required.ExternallyThe Chapel occupies a prominent position within the village, set behind decorative iron railings with a small, enclosed frontage overlooking the green. A pathway to the south side leads to the main entrance, while a further narrow strip of land to the north side provides additional external amenity spaceAgents NoteThe property is currently classified as ‘Offices and Premises’ for business rates purposes.Preliminary plans have been prepared by the current owner for conversion to a single-storey residential dwelling, though no formal application has yet been submitted.Local InformationThe small and picturesque village of Wall lies just three miles north of Hexham, on the edge of the stunning Northumberland National Park. Named after its proximity to Hadrian’s Wall, the village is steeped in Roman history and offers a peaceful base from which to explore this unesco World Heritage Site. Surrounded by rolling countryside, Wall enjoys far-reaching views and a tranquil setting, with easy access to walking and cycling routes along the Wall and through the Tyne Valley. Nearby Hexham offers full amenities including shops, restaurants, and transport links.Hexham station provides regular rail services to Newcastle (approx. 35 mins), Carlisle, and beyond. The A69 offers fast road access to Newcastle (approx. 25 m

The garden enjoys open views and makes the most of afternoon and evening light. The kitchen benefits from natural light, dining space and shaker-style units, complementing the open-plan layout. There is also a separate utility room in the hallway.There are two double bedrooms. The master bedroom overlooks the garden, while the second double bedroom includes built-in wardrobes. The bathroom offers a shower over the bath and a heated towel rail.There is also parking for two.Kirkharle is known for its green spaces and walking routes, with countryside paths and routes easily accessible from the bungalow. Kirkharle Courtyard is within walking distance and features The Running Fox bakery and café, as well as regular park runs at weekends, providing a local hub for leisure and socialising.For wider amenities, residents can drive to nearby villages and into Newcastle upon Tyne for shopping, services and employment. The A696 offers road connections towards Newcastle and the wider region, including access to Newcastle International Airport. Rail services are available from Newcastle, offering routes to Edinburgh, London and other major cities, with typical journey times to London from around three hours, making longer-distance travel practical while enjoying a rural setting.Living Room: 12'03'' x 15'03'' - 3.73m x 4.65mDining Room: 10'05'' x 6'10'' - 3.18m x 2.08mKitchen: 10'11'' x 11'09'' - 3.33m x 3.58mBedroom One: 10'07'' x 12'01'' - 3.22m x 3.68mBedroom Two: 10'09'' (+ wardrobes) x 8'10'' - 3.28m x 2.69mBathroom: 7'02'' x 5'05'' - 2.18m x 1.65mUtility.Primary services supplyElectricity: MainsWater: MainsSewerage: MainsHeating: OilBroadband: FibreMobile Signal Coverage Blackspot: NoParking: DrivewayMiningThe North East region is famous for its rich mining heritage and therefore it will be beneficial to conduct a mining search. properties.title: 3 bed semi-detached for sale
properties.description: Situated up North Bank, a prestigious location in Haydon Bridge, this well-appointed, spacious semi-detached family home has ...Listed on 30th Aug 2025Off Street ParkingAttractive Views
properties.fullDescription: Situated up North Bank, a prestigious location in Haydon Bridge, this well-appointed, spacious semi-detached family home has ample outside space from which to enjoy the elevated views over the Tyne Valley.The property is entered from the private driveway into an entrance vestibule situated to the front elevation. A further door leads through into the central hallway offering staircase to first floor and doorways to all main areas of the ground floor. A storage cupboard can also be found adjacent to the vestibule offering plenty of space for coats and boots.The lounge is situated to the front with an impressive traditional bay window, increasing the already spacious feel of the room. Chinmey breast housing feature stove and double sliding doors create a versatile opening to the rear into the dining room. This large doorway creates an open plan feeling to the space when opened fully.The dining room is another spacious room with large picture window to the rear overlooking the fields and views beyond. Offering space enough for a family dining set as well as other furniture and another feature fireplace, this is a great room with a range of uses.The kitchen is fitted with a great range of wall and base units, contrasting worktops, stainless sink unit with full drainer and mixer tap, as well as space and plumbing for appliances.Beyond the kitchen is the star of the house – the garden room. An extension of the ground floor with panoramic picture windows taking massive advantage of the elevated position of the property with views across the rural Tyne Valley. This room occupies a south facing position and is currently used as an office space with lounge furniture for evening enjoyment. Double patio doors also open out into the garden area for ultimate summer entertainment space.An additional utility room is also incorporated into the extensions housing the boiler, useful cupboard space and all major utilities.The staircase from the central hallway lead us up to the first-floor landing which offers doors to all bedrooms and bathrooms.Bedrooms one and two are very spacious and both offer fitted wardrobes/storage space as well as windows to the front and rear elevations respectively.Bedroom three is a small double with a window to the front elevation.To the rear is the family bathroom housing bath with shower over, hand wash basin and linen cupboard. Adjacent to the bathroom is a handy separate WC, ruling out some family morning frustrations over who needs to use the bathroom.Externally this property boasts a plot large enough for a potential additional dwelling which makes this garden space very attractive. Large level lawn with mature borders and far-reaching views. Driveway parking for a couple of cars as well as the detached garage with roller door. To the rear, there is a private patio area overlooking the fields behind and to the front, a low maintenance gravelled garden area. A real plus point is the patio terrace outside the garden room doors which
